description_long: This excavator features a robust yellow body with a large operator cab situated on the left side, equipped with blue-tinted windows for visibility. The machine is mounted on a pair of wide, black steel tracks, providing stability and traction for maneuvering on rough urban terrain. Its mono boom arm extends outward, supporting a standard digging bucket designed for earthmoving and construction tasks. The upper structure includes detailed venting and access panels, as well as a visible exhaust stack and handrails for maintenance access. The bucket and boom are articulated, allowing for a wide range of motion during operation. The overall design is typical of heavy-duty construction vehicles used for excavation, demolition, and material handling in urban environments.
description: A yellow tracked excavator with a mono boom, left-side cab, and standard digging bucket, designed for heavy-duty urban construction tasks. Features include blue-tinted windows, black steel tracks, and detailed upper structure components.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the mono boom, bucket, and left-positioned cab with blue window.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the width of the tracks, the back of the upper structure, and the cab's rear profile.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the boom, bucket, and right side of the upper structure with venting details.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the bucket, the boom's alignment, and the cab's front window.
